description: The Judiciary of Iran (Qovveh-ye Qaza'iyeh) is the judicial branch of the Islamic Republic, headed by a Chief Justice appointed directly by the [Office of the Supreme Leader](/orgs/office-of-the-supreme-leader) for five-year terms. The Chief Justice also nominates the six jurist members of the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council). The Judiciary has been a primary instrument of political repression, overseeing prosecutions of reformists, journalists, and human rights defenders. Chief Justices since 1989 include [Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani](/people/akbar-hashemi-rafsanjani) (briefly), Mohammad Yazdi (1989–1999), Mahmoud Hashemi Shahroudi (1999–2009), [Sadegh Amoli Larijani](/people/sadegh-larijani) (2009–2019), [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi) (2019–2021), and [Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Ejei](/people/gholam-hossein-mohseni-ejei) (2021–present).
editorial_notes: Chief Justice appointed by Supreme Leader for 5-year renewable terms. Nominator of the 6 jurist members of the Guardian Council, giving the Supreme Leader indirect control over all 12 GC seats.
